title: Nursery memory and teaching-bed readability
summary: Use the nursery as a quiet memory of places visited, not a chore loop or base-building system.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Nursery mature/active states communicate plant state, ecological memory, and utility without overlap or repeated route hints.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Harden nursery layout/state helpers only where proven overlap or priority bugs remain; do not widen the nursery shell.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_346_target: Keep lane 1 test-first: update the live nursery-page seam test for lane 2's reviewed salmonberry memory line and add one mature-bed priority regression so habitat memory cannot overlap repeated route hints.
scout_refinement.baseline_failure: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-station-nursery-page.test.ts` currently fails because `resolveMatureBedFooterCopy(...)` returns `Cool forest edge where salmonberry thickets hold shade.` while the live-page test expects the old salmonberry footer.
main_acceptance: mature salmonberry live-page footer expectation uses `Cool forest edge where salmonberry thickets hold shade.`
coverage proves mature teaching-bed memory keeps priority over the route-support strip without widening the nursery page
runtime code remains unchanged unless the focused test exposes an actual helper bug
no save/schema, route-support behavior, nursery shell layout, new station card, new page, geometry, or copy-bank changes

main_result.summary: Updated the live nursery-page seam test for the reviewed salmonberry mature memory line and added a regression proving mature teaching-bed memory suppresses an available route-support hint without changing nursery layout or runtime behavior.

critic_result.summary: The lane-1 pass stayed test-only, corrected the reviewed salmonberry mature footer expectation, and proved an available route-support hint stays hidden while mature teaching-bed memory owns the nursery page.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Refresh one teaching-bed memory family so it reflects a route place and science relationship in one short line.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Add or tune a tiny nursery visual proof that makes growth visible before copy is read.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_348_target: Expose and test the existing nursery home-place strip state so active teaching-bed growth reads visually before copy is read, without changing nursery layout or adding another surface.
existing_seam: `drawNurseryHomePlaceStrip(...)` already draws the bottom strip on active/mature beds.
The strip already has four stage pips plus log-pile and pollinator-patch motifs from existing nursery extras.
The visual contract is currently implicit inside drawing code and needs a small pure seam/test proof.
main_acceptance: add or expose a tiny tested nursery home-place strip state seam using existing NurseryStateView data
prove locked/ready teaching-bed states do not show the strip
prove stocked/rooting/growing/mature bed states map to visual progress 1/2/3/4
prove existing nursery extras can light the log-pile and pollinator-patch motifs without changing reward or route-support behavior
keep mature-bed memory priority and existing nursery page dimensions intact
no nursery copy, route-support resolver, station page, save schema, route definition, route filing behavior, geometry, or science content changes
if drawing itself changes, add ignored 256x160 browser proof under output/lane-3-main-348-browser; helper/test-only visual-equivalent work does not require browser proof

main_result.summary: Exposed the existing nursery home-place strip as a tiny pure state seam and wired the current strip drawing through it without changing the visual recipe.
guardrails: No nursery copy, layout, route-support resolver, station page, save schema, route definition, route filing, world geometry, or science content changes.
Locked and ready beds keep the strip hidden; active and mature beds keep the existing bottom-strip visual family.
Existing reward-backed nursery extras continue to light the log-pile and pollinator-patch motifs through the same saved extra state.

critic_result.summary: The nursery home-place strip seam is behavior-equivalent, test-backed, and preserves the existing mature-memory, route-support, layout, save, station, geometry, copy, and science boundaries.
critic_result.packet_hygiene: Moved the lane-3 visual-growth scout refinement out of the lane-2 contract block and into lane-3 so future agents read the handoff in the correct lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Verify nursery support clues resolve by active route beat and never flatten multiple routes into the same repeated hint.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_349_target: Add a tiny beat-specific nursery support hint resolver and focused tests so route-support clues follow the active route beat instead of the first claimed support project for the whole route.
current_gap: `edge-pattern-line` already maps active beats to dune-lupine, salmonberry, or mountain-avens support.
`coastal-shelter-line` and `treeline-shelter-line` still fall back to the first claimed route-support project for the whole route.
Tests cover the edge-route happy path but not all route families or no-substitution cases.
main_acceptance: centralize route-support hint selection around routeId plus activeBeatId instead of scanning the first claimed project for the whole route
if the active beat's mapped reward is not claimed, show no route-support hint rather than substituting another beat's support
keep the capstone salmonberry fallback only for the no-active-beat post-route window
preserve mature teaching-bed memory priority and existing nursery page layout
add focused nursery tests for coastal switching, treeline no-early-avens, existing edge mapping, and at least one no-substitution case
do not change nursery layout, authored plant copy, station pages, save schema, route definitions, route filing behavior, world geometry, or science content
verification: `npm test -- --run src/test/field-station-nursery-page.test.ts` if nursery page priority assertions are touched

main_result.summary: Added a beat-specific nursery route-support resolver so coastal, treeline, and edge route hints resolve from the active beat and never substitute another claimed support when the mapped beat reward is missing.
guardrails: No nursery layout, station page, save schema, route definition, route filing, world geometry, or science content changes.
The existing salmonberry capstone fallback stays limited to the no-active-beat post-route window.
Mature teaching-bed memory priority remains unchanged.

critic_result.summary: The route-support resolver now keys nursery hints to the active route beat, covers coastal switching, treeline no-early-avens, existing edge behavior, no-substitution, and the no-active-beat salmonberry capstone fallback without widening nursery layout, station pages, save/schema, route definitions, route filing, geometry, or science content.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

target.current_line: Cool wet edge tucked under taller cover.
target.recommended_line: Cool forest edge where salmonberry thickets hold shade.

science_support: docs/science-source-ledger.md verifies `salmonberry` as a Pacific coastal forest-edge species.
src/content/shared-entries.ts says salmonberry grows in moist coastal forests and edges and dense stems build sheltered thickets.
src/content/biomes/coastal-scrub.ts and src/content/biomes/forest.ts already use salmonberry and berry-edge notes to teach cooler, wetter shade at the scrub-to-forest transition.

main_347_scope.required_change: Refresh only `salmonberry-bed.memorySummary` and its focused mature-bed test expectations.
main_347_scope.shared_file_reason: `NURSERY_PROJECT_DEFINITIONS` currently lives in src/engine/nursery.ts, so this lane-2 pass may touch that shared engine file for authored copy only.

non_goals: No nursery layout, station state, route-support selection, save schema, route controller behavior, world geometry, or renderer changes.
No new nursery card, notebook page, station panel, route hint, or broad memory-line rewrite.
No science-source ledger update unless implementation copy drifts beyond the existing verified salmonberry support.

lane_2_main_result.implemented_line: Cool forest edge where salmonberry thickets hold shade.
lane_2_main_result.guardrail_result: No nursery layout, station state, route-support selection, save schema, route controller behavior, renderer structure, world geometry, or new UI surface changed.

reviewed_scope: focused nursery test expectation and anchors
